context.project_description: Implement LLM token counting instrumentation that captures input/output tokens from chat, embedding, and reranking calls across multiple providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) by wrapping provider methods with existing InstrumentedProxy infrastructure.
key_requirements: Wrap chat/embedding/rerank methods in all LLM provider classes with InstrumentedProxy using maybe_wrap helper
Implement TokenTrackingHook that logs structured token usage information via existing logging framework
Extract token counts from response metadata for providers that support it (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere)
Estimate tokens for providers without direct counting (Ollama) based on prompt length or model specs
constraints: Cannot modify fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py - must leverage existing InstrumentedProxy and hook infrastructure
Must use existing logging system with get_logger function for structured token usage logs
No new file creation beyond extending current instrumentation approach in provider classes
Token counting implementation must not disrupt normal operation or add significant performance overhead
context.existing_context: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py contains InstrumentedProxy and BenchmarkHook protocols with register_hook/unregister_hook functions, fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py defines protocol interfaces for LLM providers, fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py, anthropic.py, cohere.py, ollama.py contain concrete implementations of these protocols
stakeholders: Developer tasked with implementing token tracking for LLM providers
System performance engineer concerned about latency impact

in_scope: Tracking input and output tokens for chat models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere)
Logging structured token usage via existing logging infrastructure
Wrapping provider methods using existing InstrumentedProxy and maybe_wrap helpers
out_of_scope: Adding new instrumentation modules or files beyond extending current infrastructure
Modifying core instrumentation logic in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py
Implementing exact tokenization for Ollama (only estimation via prompt length)
Aggregating tokens per user query rather than per individual call

existing_files: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — Instrumentation system for benchmarking plugin performance
fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py — Provider protocols for LLM clients
fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py — OpenAI provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py — Anthropic provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py — Cohere provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py — Ollama provider wrappers using direct HTTP calls
fitz_ai/logging/logger.py — Unified logging setup for the entire Fitz project
needed_artifacts: fitz_ai/llm/instrumentation/token_counter.py — Core token counting logic for different LLM providers implementing a hook that captures input/output tokens from each provider call

fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — Add token tracking to benchmark hooks or introduce a TokenTrackingHook protocol if needed, and extend InstrumentedProxy._wrap_method() to handle token counting where applicable

assumptions.assumption: We're tracking input + output tokens for chat models (OpenAI/Anthropic/Cohere), embedding token counts where provided, and estimating for Ollama
assumptions.impact: Could either over-count or under-count depending on how 'tokens' are defined by business stakeholders

assumptions.assumption: Token usage should be logged via the existing logging infrastructure with structured fields
assumptions.impact: Might need to switch to metrics store (Prometheus, Datadog) instead of logs for better aggregation later

assumptions.assumption: The instrumentation system already supports custom hooks that can be registered at runtime to capture token usage
assumptions.impact: May need to modify `InstrumentedProxy._wrap_method` or create new hook interfaces if the existing infrastructure is insufficient

assumptions.assumption: For Ollama and local models, we'll use model-specific estimations (e.g., based on prompt length) rather than exact counting
assumptions.impact: Could provide misleading information if exact tokenization is required

assumptions.assumption: Per-provider method call level tracking is sufficient and matches user's stated intent to 'see how many LLM tokens each query costs'
assumptions.impact: May want per-query aggregation rather than individual calls if users care about overall cost of a single question

assumptions.assumption: Token counting overhead is minimal and not a significant concern for production use
assumptions.impact: Could cause noticeable latency or require lazy evaluation of token counts only when needed (e.g., in development mode)

approaches.name: Extend Provider Protocols with Usage Metadata
approaches.description: Modify base LLM provider protocols to return structured response objects that include token usage metadata alongside content. This requires changing ChatProvider.chat() from returning str to returning a structured object with prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, and total_tokens fields.
pros: Provides accurate token tracking by design
cons: Must update all concrete provider implementations (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama)
Increases complexity in response handling throughout the call stack
best_for: Systems where token accounting is critical for cost management and optimization

approaches.name: Instrumentation-Based Token Tracking with HTTP Interception
approaches.description: Implement a middleware or proxy layer that intercepts LLM API calls at the HTTP level to extract token usage from provider responses (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere) before they reach existing provider implementations. This approach requires monitoring network traffic and parsing raw API responses for usage data.
pros: Can work with all current providers without modification
Captures actual token consumption from LLM APIs
cons: Requires significant infrastructure changes to add HTTP-level monitoring
Difficult to implement consistently across different provider API formats
Potential performance overhead due to network interception and parsing
best_for: Legacy systems where modifying protocols is not feasible but token tracking is required

architecture.recommended: Extend Provider Protocols with Usage Metadata
architecture.reasoning: The proposed approach of adding token usage tracking assumes that LLM providers return structured responses with .usage metadata, but current implementations only return raw strings. The base ChatProvider.chat() signature returns str and all concrete providers (OpenAIChat, AnthropicChat, etc.) implement this contract without exposing token data. To make the proposal feasible, we must extend the protocol interfaces to support returning usage information in a consistent way across all providers. While both approaches would require substantial changes, extending protocols is more aligned with existing architectural patterns in the codebase (such as the instrumentation system which already uses Protocol-based contracts) and allows for clean integration with future observability features. The HTTP interception approach may work but introduces significant complexity through network monitoring, potential performance impacts, and inconsistency across provider APIs that don't uniformly expose usage.

key_tradeoffs.protocol_extension_vs_instrumentation: Extending LLM provider protocols to return structured response objects with token metadata provides clean, type-safe access but requires breaking changes to existing APIs; using instrumentation hooks avoids API modifications but cannot retroactively add metadata to string-returning methods
key_tradeoffs.tracking_accuracy_vs_performance: Accurate token tracking requires parsing and exposing usage data from each LLM call which adds computational overhead; simpler approaches like heuristic estimation or sampling reduce performance impact but sacrifice accuracy
key_tradeoffs.centralized_vs_decentralized_tracking: Centralized token aggregation provides unified reporting but requires significant architectural changes to pipeline components; decentralized approach via individual strategy tracking is less invasive but may miss some interactions and complicate correlation

technology_considerations: LLM provider protocols need modification to return structured response objects with usage metadata instead of raw strings
Instrumentation system must be extended with token-specific hooks or modified to capture HTTP-level response data from API calls
Answer object class needs extension with token_usage field to store and expose tracking information to SDK callers
LLM client factory should support conditional wrapping of providers for token tracking based on configuration settings
TokenUsageTracker service is required to aggregate and correlate token counts across multiple LLM interactions within single query processing
architecture.scope_statement: Modify base LLM provider protocols to return structured response objects containing token metadata, update all concrete implementations (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) to extract and include this information in their API responses, extend the Answer class to store token usage data, and integrate with existing instrumentation hooks for aggregation across multiple LLM calls within a single query.

adrs.title: ADR: Extend LLM Provider Protocols for Token Usage Tracking
adrs.context: The FitzKrag engine needs to track and report token usage per query to enable cost monitoring, optimization, and user transparency. Currently, all LLM provider protocols (ChatProvider, StreamingChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider) only return raw response content without any metadata about tokens consumed.
adrs.decision: Extend base provider protocols with structured response objects that include token usage information, or add optional methods to retrieve usage data after calls. Modify Answer objects to include token_usage field for aggregation and reporting.
adrs.rationale: This approach maintains the existing abstraction layer while enabling comprehensive token tracking across all LLM interactions. It follows established patterns in the codebase (dataclasses, protocol extensions) and allows for backward compatibility through optional fields or new interfaces that can be gradually adopted.
consequences: Breaking change to LLM provider interface contracts requiring updates to all concrete implementations
Need to modify Answer object definition to include token_usage field with proper typing
Extension of InstrumentedProxy system and BenchmarkHook protocol may be required for seamless tracking integration
Potential performance overhead from parsing additional metadata in responses
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1: Add separate tracking mechanism outside provider calls - rejected because it would require invasive changes to HTTP client layers and wouldn't capture all token usage points
Alternative 2: Modify existing protocols only for streaming providers - rejected because it creates inconsistent handling where some LLM interactions don't track usage while others do
Alternative 3: Implement external monitoring via HTTP interceptors - rejected because this adds significant complexity and breaks the clean abstraction layer

adrs.title: ADR: Extending Provider Protocols with Usage Metadata
adrs.context: The FitzKragEngine needs to track token usage across different LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) but current ChatProvider.chat() interface only returns raw strings without exposing usage metadata. The system must maintain backward compatibility while enabling consistent usage tracking.
adrs.decision: Modify the base ChatProvider protocol to return a structured response object that includes both the chat response content and usage metadata, rather than just a plain string
adrs.rationale: The current implementation assumes providers will always return structured data with .usage metadata, but in reality all concrete implementations only return raw strings. To make token tracking feasible across all providers, we need to establish a consistent interface contract that can be implemented by both existing and future providers while preserving backward compatibility where possible.
consequences: Breaking change for existing provider implementations that currently return str instead of structured response
Adds complexity to the protocol as it must handle both successful responses with usage data and error cases without usage metadata
Requires all client code using ChatProvider to update to handle the new structured response format
alternatives_considered: Keep current string-based interface and add separate token tracking mechanism that operates on raw responses (but this doesn't integrate well with existing provider abstractions)
Add a separate method like get_chat_with_usage() alongside chat() to avoid breaking changes, but this creates redundancy and inconsistency in the API
Introduce an optional metadata field in the response interface that can be ignored by providers not supporting it (but this breaks type safety and leads to runtime errors)

artifacts.content:
from __future__ import annotations

dataclass RerankResult:
    document: str
    relevance_score: float
    index: int


Protocol ChatProvider:
    def chat(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str


Protocol StreamingChatProvider:
    def chat_stream(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> Iterator[str]


Protocol EmbeddingProvider:
    def embed(self, text: str) -> list[float]
    def embed_batch(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]
    def dimensions(self) -> int


Protocol RerankProvider:
    def rerank(self, query: str, documents: list[str], top_n: int | None) -> list[RerankResult]


Protocol VisionProvider:
    def describe_image(self, image_base64: str, prompt: str | None) -> str
artifacts.purpose: Base provider protocols defining the interface contracts for LLM clients including chat, embedding, reranking, and vision capabilities
Instrumentation system for benchmarking and monitoring plugin performance with hook-based interception

from __future__ import annotations

dataclass RerankResult:
    document: str
    relevance_score: float
    index: int


Protocol ChatProvider:
    def chat(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str


Protocol StreamingChatProvider:
    def chat_stream(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> Iterator[str]


Protocol EmbeddingProvider:
    def embed(self, text: str) -> list[float]
    def embed_batch(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]
    def dimensions(self) -> int


Protocol RerankProvider:
    def rerank(self, query: str, documents: list[str], top_n: int | None) -> list[RerankResult]


Protocol VisionProvider:
    def describe_image(self, image_base64: str, prompt: str | None) -> str

from __future__ import annotations
import concurrent.futures
import hashlib
import logging
import pathlib
import re

artifacts.purpose: FitzKragEngine - Knowledge Routing Augmented Generation engine implementing the core query answering logic
Fitz class - Stateful SDK for the Fitz KRAG framework providing convenient access to engine functionality

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
deliverables: Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py
phases.verification_command: pytest tests/llm/providers/test_base.py tests/llm/providers/test_openai.py tests/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py tests/llm/providers/test_cohere.py tests/llm/providers/test_ollama.py -v

phases.name: Create Token Usage Hook
phases.objective: Define a benchmark hook to capture and log LLM token usage from provider responses
deliverables: New module fitz_ai/core/token_tracking.py implementing BenchmarkHook protocol
on_call_start method that stores call context for tracking
on_call_end method that parses usage data from API responses and logs tokens
key_risks: Providers without explicit usage reporting (e.g. Ollama) may require heuristic estimation

phases.name: Instrument LLM Provider Methods
phases.objective: Wrap provider instances with InstrumentedProxy to intercept method calls and track token usage
deliverables: Updated provider wrapping logic in fitz_ai/llm/client.py or factory modules
Instrumentation of chat, embed, rerank, vision methods across all supported providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama)
key_risks: Streaming APIs (chat_stream) are not instrumented due to complexity of mid-stream token capture

phases.name: Register Token Tracking Hook
phases.objective: Integrate the token tracking hook into the LLM client initialization flow
deliverables: Updated factory or bootstrapping logic in fitz_ai/llm/factory.py to register TokenUsageHook during startup
Configuration of token tracking as optional feature with default disabled
key_risks: Hook registration may conflict with existing hook systems if not properly isolated

phases.name: Handle Edge Cases and Fallbacks
phases.objective: Implement fallback mechanisms for providers without explicit token usage reporting
deliverables: Heuristic-based token estimation logic for local LLMs like Ollama
Documentation of supported models and their token tracking capabilities
key_risks: Approximate heuristics may introduce significant variance in token count estimates

risks.description: Not all LLM providers expose detailed token usage data, particularly local models like Ollama which may lack explicit usage reporting fields.
risks.mitigation: Implement fallback mechanisms for token estimation based on input length or approximate model-specific heuristics for providers without direct usage reporting.
risks.contingency: If a provider does not return usage data, log an informational message indicating that token counting is not available and use estimated values where possible.
risks.verification: Verify through testing with both OpenAI/Cohere (with usage) and Ollama (without usage) to confirm fallback behavior.

risks.description: Streaming APIs are not instrumented for token logging due to complexity of capturing tokens mid-stream, which may lead to incomplete tracking.
risks.mitigation: Document limitation that streaming APIs do not track token usage and focus instrumentation on synchronous methods (chat, embed).
risks.contingency: Add a warning in logs when streaming operations are detected, indicating that token counting is not supported for such calls.
risks.verification: Validate that chat_stream methods are excluded from instrumentation while regular chat method gets proper tracking.

risks.description: The new token tracking mechanism may introduce performance overhead during LLM calls due to additional hook execution and logging.
risks.mitigation: Implement sampling or conditional logging based on environment flags (e.g., only log in development/staging).
risks.contingency: Add a toggle switch to enable/disable token tracking via configuration so it can be turned off for production if needed.
risks.verification: Measure latency differences with/without instrumentation enabled using benchmark tests.

risks.description: Potential inconsistency in token counting across different providers due to varying SDK responses and reporting formats.
risks.mitigation: Create a consistent wrapper or adapter layer that normalizes usage data from all provider types before logging.
risks.contingency: Log raw response data alongside normalized token count for debugging purposes when discrepancies occur.
risks.verification: Cross-check token values across providers by comparing similar queries and verifying summation logic.

recommended_contingencies: Implement fallback estimation for models without explicit usage reporting (e.g., Ollama)
Add warning logs when streaming APIs are called to indicate missing token tracking support
Provide configurable toggle to disable token tracking in production environments
